Implement the following plan:

# Plan: Quote UI Improvements & Blueprint Quote Type

## Context

The current quote system uses a **line-items model** (description × qty × unit price). The Texas Ribs example quote follows a completely different pattern — a **project blueprint** with narrative sections (problem, opportunity, deliverables, timeline, fixed pricing with optional preferential rate, and next steps). This plan introduces a second quote type and redesigns the viewer to match real-world consulting proposals.

---

## Part 1 — Data Model Changes

### Add `quoteType` discriminator to `Quote`

**File:** `src/features/quotes/types/index.ts`

```ts
export type QuoteType = "line-items" | "blueprint";
```

Add to `Quote` interface:
```ts
quoteType: QuoteType;
```

### New optional fields for `blueprint` type

Add to `Quote` interface:
```ts
// Blueprint-only fields
problem?: string;           // Narrative: El Problema
opportunity?: string;       // Narrative: La Oportunidad
deliverables?: Deliverable[];
timeline?: string;          // e.g. "3 semanas"
fixedPrice?: number;        // The price to charge
listPrice?: number;         // Original/crossed-out price (optional)
preferentialNote?: string;  // e.g. "Por tratarse de la familia Perezcano"
paymentTerms?: string;      // e.g. "50% al arranque, 50% a la entrega"
nextSteps?: string[];       // Ordered action items
scopeNote?: string;         // Scope/implementation disclaimer
```

New `Deliverable` type:
```ts
export interface Deliverable {
  id: string;
  number: number;
  title: string;
  description: string;
}
```

### Schema update

**File:** `src/features/quotes/lib/schemas.ts`

Add `deliverableSchema` and extend `quoteFormSchema` with the new optional fields. Use `.optional()` for all blueprint fields. Add `quoteType` as a required field with default `"line-items"`.

### Database migration

**File:** `src/shared/lib/db.ts`

Add new columns to the quotes table:
- `quote_type TEXT DEFAULT 'line-items'`
- `problem TEXT`
- `opportunity TEXT`
- `deliverables TEXT` (JSON array)
- `timeline TEXT`
- `fixed_price REAL`
- `list_price REAL`
- `preferential_note TEXT`
- `payment_terms TEXT`
- `next_steps TEXT` (JSON array)
- `scope_note TEXT`

Update `getAllQuotes()`, `getQuoteById()`, `createQuote()`, `updateQuote()` in `src/features/quotes/lib/queries.ts` to handle these fields with JSON parse/stringify for array fields.

---

## Part 2 — QuoteViewer UI

**File:** `src/features/quotes/components/QuoteViewer.tsx`

Branch rendering on `quote.quoteType`:
- `"line-items"` → existing layout (no changes)
- `"blueprint"` → new layout described below

### Blueprint layout sections (in order)

**1. Header** — same as today (PRAGMA logo, language toggle)

**2. Title block** — status badge, title, quote ID, date. No line-item description here.

**3. Problem card** (if `problem` set)
```
[ CARD ]
  tiny label: "EL PROBLEMA" / "THE PROBLEM"
  body text (prose)
```
Visual: left cyan accent border (`border-l-2 border-pragma-accent`), Card component.

**4. Opportunity card** (if `opportunity` set)
```
[ CARD ]
  tiny label: "LA OPORTUNIDAD" / "THE OPPORTUNITY"
  body text with italic callout for the metric/savings note
```

**5. Divider `<hr>`**

**6. Deliverables list** (if `deliverables` set)
Each deliverable as a card row:
```
[ bold number ]  Title — description
```
Number in `text-pragma-accent`, title bold, description in `text-pragma-muted`.

**7. Divider `<hr>`**

**8. Timeline + Investment side-by-side (sm:flex-row)**

Timeline:
```
[ CARD ]
  label: "TIMELINE"
  large text: "3 semanas"
```

Investment:
```
[ CARD with cyan glow border ]
  label: "INVERSIÓN"
  list_price (if set): ~~$60,000 MXN~~  (line-through, muted)
  preferential_note (if set): small italic line
  fixed_price: large bold text-pragma-accent  "$35,000 MXN"
  payment_terms: small muted text below
```

**9. Scope note** (if set) — small italic muted paragraph, no card.

**10. Next Steps** (if set)
```
[ CARD ]
  label: "SIGUIENTES PASOS"
  ordered list: 1. 2. 3. ...
```

**11. Actions** — same PDF download + accept button as today

---

## Part 3 — QuoteForm

**File:** `src/features/quotes/components/QuoteForm.tsx`

Add a **Quote Type toggle** at the top of the form:
```
[ Line Items ]  [ Blueprint ]
```
Use local React state to control which fields render.

- When `line-items`: current form unchanged
- When `blueprint`:
  - Hide line items table
  - Show: Problem (textarea), Opportunity (textarea)
  - Deliverables: dynamic list — title + description per deliverable, add/remove buttons (same pattern as current line items)
  - Timeline (text input)
  - Fixed Price (number input + currency selector)
  - List Price (number input, optional — "original price for strikethrough")
  - Preferential Note (text input, optional)
  - Payment Terms (text input)
  - Next Steps: dynamic list of text inputs, add/remove buttons
  - Scope Note (textarea, optional)

---

## Part 4 — PDF Document

**File:** `src/features/quotes/components/QuotePdfDocument.tsx`

Add a second layout branch for blueprint quotes:
- Replace line-items table with deliverables list
- Show problem/opportunity as text sections
- Show timeline + investment block
- Show next steps as numbered list

---

## Execution Order

1. `types/index.ts` — add `Deliverable`, `QuoteType`, extend `Quote`
2. `schemas.ts` — add validation for new fields
3. `db.ts` + `queries.ts` — DB schema + CRUD update
4. `QuoteViewer.tsx` — blueprint rendering branch
5. `QuoteForm.tsx` — quote type toggle + blueprint fields
6. `QuotePdfDocument.tsx` — blueprint PDF layout
7. Seed data — add one blueprint-type example quote

---

## Verification

1. Run `GET /api/dev/seed` to populate DB with a blueprint quote
2. Visit `/en/quote/[blueprint-id]` — verify blueprint layout renders correctly with all sections
3. Visit `/en/admin/quotes/new` — toggle between line-items and blueprint, verify correct fields appear
4. Create a blueprint quote via the form, save, verify it appears in admin table
5. Download PDF for a blueprint quote — verify sections render correctly
6. Existing line-items quotes unchanged — open an existing quote and confirm no regression

---

## Files Modified

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/features/quotes/types/index.ts` | Add `Deliverable`, `QuoteType`, extend `Quote` |
| `src/features/quotes/lib/schemas.ts` | Add blueprint field validation |
| `src/shared/lib/db.ts` | Add new columns |
| `src/features/quotes/lib/queries.ts` | Update CRUD for new fields |
| `src/features/quotes/components/QuoteViewer.tsx` | Blueprint layout branch |
| `src/features/quotes/components/QuoteForm.tsx` | Quote type toggle + blueprint fields |
| `src/features/quotes/components/QuotePdfDocument.tsx` | Blueprint PDF layout |
| `src/app/api/dev/seed/route.ts` | Add blueprint seed quote |

```ts
export type QuoteType = "line-items" | "blueprint";
```

Add to `Quote` interface:

```ts
quoteType: QuoteType;
```

### New optional fields for `blueprint` type

```ts
export interface Deliverable {
  id: string;
```

  },
];

export async function GET() {
  if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== "development") {
    return new NextResponse("Not Found", { status: 404 });
  }

  const results = await Promise.all(SAMPLE_QUOTES.map((q) => createQuote(q)));

  const quotes = results.map((q) => ({
    id: q.id,
    title: q.title,
    type: q.quoteType,
    status: q.status,
    client: q.clientName,
    viewUrl: `/${q.locale}/quote/${q.id}`,
  }));

  return NextResponse.json({
    message: `Seeded ${quotes.length} sample quotes`,
    quotes,
  });
}

Everything looks correct. Here's a summary of all changes made:

**8 files modified, all TypeScript-clean:**

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `types/index.ts` | Added `QuoteType`, `Deliverable`, extended `Quote` with 10 optional blueprint fields |
| `lib/schemas.ts` | Added `deliverableSchema`, relaxed `lineItems` min(1), added all blueprint field validations |
| `shared/lib/db.ts` | Added new columns to CREATE TABLE + migration loop for existing DBs |
| `lib/queries.ts` | Updated `rowToQuote`, `createQuote`, `updateQuote` for all new fields |
| `actions/index.ts` | Added `extractBlueprintFields()` helper, updated both action handlers |
| `messages/en.json` + `es.json` | Added 8 Quote keys + 13 Admin keys for blueprint UI |
| `QuoteViewer.tsx` | Extracted `QuoteHeader`/`QuoteActions` shared components; added full blueprint layout branch |
| `QuoteForm.tsx` | Added quote type toggle, deliverables dynamic list, next steps dynamic list, all blueprint fields |
| `QuotePdfDocument.tsx` | Added `BlueprintPdfDocument` component with full PDF layout |
| `seed/route.ts` | Added Texas Ribs blueprint quote with all fields populated |

**To verify:** Hit `GET /api/dev/seed` in dev mode, then visit the blueprint quote URL — it will render the narrative proposal layout instead of the line-items table.

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user requested implementation of a detailed plan titled "Quote UI Improvements & Blueprint Quote Type." The plan introduced a second quote type (`blueprint`) alongside the existing `line-items` model. Blueprint quotes follow a consulting proposal pattern with narrative sections (problem, opportunity, deliverables, timeline, fixed pricing with optional list price strikethrough, preferential note, payment terms, next steps, scope note). The plan covered 8 files across data model, DB schema, CRUD, server actions, UI components, PDF generation, and seed data. After implementation the user committed, pushed to GitHub, started the dev server, and asked for the admin password.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Next.js 16 App Router with server actions
   - next-intl for i18n (en/es locales)
   - libSQL/Turso (`@libsql/client`) for persistence
   - SQLite ALTER TABLE migration pattern (try/catch per column)
   - Zod schema validation with discriminated optional fields
   - `@react-pdf/renderer` for PDF generation
   - React controlled state for dynamic form lists (deliverables, nextSteps, lineItems)
   - TypeScript discriminated union on `quoteType: "line-items" | "blueprint"`
   - `nanoid` for ID generation
   - Tailwind v4 dark design system (pragma-accent, pragma-muted, pragma-border, etc.)
   - `ADMIN_PASSWORD` env var for admin auth

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`src/features/quotes/types/index.ts`**
     - Added `QuoteType = "line-items" | "blueprint"`, `Deliverable` interface, extended `Quote` with 10 optional blueprint fields
     ```ts
     export type QuoteType = "line-items" | "blueprint";
     export interface Deliverable { id: string; number: number; title: string; description: string; }
     export interface Quote {
       // ... existing fields ...
       quoteType: QuoteType;
       problem?: string; opportunity?: string; deliverables?: Deliverable[];
       timeline?: string; fixedPrice?: number; listPrice?: number;
       preferentialNote?: string; paymentTerms?: string; nextSteps?: string[]; scopeNote?: string;
     }
     ```

   - **`src/features/quotes/lib/schemas.ts`**
     - Added `deliverableSchema`, changed `lineItems` from `.min(1)` to `.default([])`, added all blueprint fields as optional
     ```ts
     export const deliverableSchema = z.object({ id: z.string(), number: z.number().min(1), title: z.string().min(1), description: z.string().min(1) });
     export const quoteFormSchema = z.object({
       quoteType: z.enum(["line-items", "blueprint"]).default("line-items"),
       lineItems: z.array(lineItemSchema).default([]),
       problem: z.string().optional(), opportunity: z.string().optional(),
       deliverables: z.array(deliverableSchema).optional(),
       // ... all other blueprint fields ...
     });
     ```

   - **`src/shared/lib/db.ts`**
     - Updated CREATE TABLE to include all new columns upfront; added migration loop for existing DBs
     ```ts
     const migrations = [
       "ALTER TABLE quotes ADD COLUMN quote_type TEXT DEFAULT 'line-items'",
       "ALTER TABLE quotes ADD COLUMN problem TEXT",
       // ... 9 more columns
     ];
     for (const sql of migrations) { try { await db.execute(sql); } catch { /* already exists */ } }
     ```

   - **`src/features/quotes/lib/queries.ts`**
     - `rowToQuote()` updated to map all new fields; `createQuote()` and `updateQuote()` updated with expanded SQL
     - Key change: `deliverables` and `nextSteps` are JSON-serialized arrays; `fixedPrice`/`listPrice` use `?? null` for proper null handling
     - Imports now include `Deliverable` type

   - **`src/features/quotes/actions/index.ts`**
     - Added `extractBlueprintFields(formData)` helper; spread into both `createQuoteAction` and `updateQuoteAction` raw objects
     ```ts
     function extractBlueprintFields(formData: FormData) {
       return {
         problem: (formData.get("problem") as string) || undefined,
         deliverables: deliverablesRaw ? JSON.parse(deliverablesRaw) : undefined,
         fixedPrice: fixedPriceRaw ? parseFloat(fixedPriceRaw) : undefined,
         // ... etc
       };
     }
     ```

   - **`src/messages/en.json` + `src/messages/es.json`**
     - Added to `Quote` namespace: `theProblem`, `theOpportunity`, `deliverables`, `timeline`, `investment`, `originalPrice`, `nextSteps`, `scopeNote`
     - Added to `Admin` namespace: `quoteType`, `lineItemsType`, `blueprintType`, `problem`, `opportunity`, `deliverableTitle`, `deliverableDesc`, `addDeliverable`, `timeline`, `fixedPrice`, `listPrice`, `preferentialNote`, `paymentTerms`, `nextStep`, `addNextStep`, `scopeNote`

   - **`src/features/quotes/components/QuoteViewer.tsx`**
     - Refactored: extracted `QuoteHeader` and `QuoteActions` as inner shared components
     - Added `BlueprintViewer` component with sections: title block, client card, problem card (cyan left border), opportunity card (italic), deliverables list (numbered with accent color), divider, timeline+investment side-by-side (investment card has cyan glow border, strikethrough list price), scope note, next steps ordered list, actions
     - Main `QuoteViewer` branches on `quote.quoteType === "blueprint"`

   - **`src/features/quotes/components/QuoteForm.tsx`**
     - Added state: `quoteType`, `deliverables: Deliverable[]`, `nextSteps: string[]`
     - Added handlers: `addDeliverable`, `removeDeliverable`, `updateDeliverable`, `addNextStep`, `removeNextStep`, `updateNextStep`
     - Quote type toggle UI at top (two styled buttons)
     - `handleSubmit` sets `quoteType`, branches lineItems/deliverables/nextSteps appropriately
     - Blueprint fields: Problem textarea, Opportunity textarea, Deliverables dynamic list (numbered), Timeline input, fixedPrice/listPrice/preferentialNote/paymentTerms inputs, Next Steps dynamic list, Scope Note textarea

   - **`src/features/quotes/components/QuotePdfDocument.tsx`**
     - Added `BlueprintPdfDocument` component with full react-pdf layout
     - New styles: `narrativeBox`, `deliverableRow`, `timelineInvestmentRow`, `investmentBox`, `fixedPriceText`, `listPriceText` (with `textDecoration: "line-through"`), etc.
     - Main export `QuotePdfDocument` branches on `quote.quoteType === "blueprint"`

   - **`src/app/api/dev/seed/route.ts`**
     - Added `quoteType: "line-items" as const` to all existing quotes
     - Added 4th quote: Texas Ribs blueprint quote with all blueprint fields populated (problem, opportunity, 4 deliverables, timeline "3 semanas", fixedPrice 35000, listPrice 60000, preferentialNote, paymentTerms, 3 nextSteps, scopeNote)

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **TypeScript error TS1117** in `QuotePdfDocument.tsx`: "An object literal cannot have multiple properties with the same name" — the `narrativeBox` style had `paddingLeft` defined twice (first as `paddingLeft: 10`, then again as `paddingLeft: 14` after `padding: 12`). Fixed by removing the first `paddingLeft: 10`.
   - **`git add` with bracket path** failed on zsh (`no matches found: src/app/[locale]/`) — fixed by using `git add -A` instead.

5. Problem Solving:
   - **Migration strategy**: Since `initDb` uses `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS`, new columns wouldn't be added to existing DBs. Solved with a migration loop that runs `ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN` for each new column and silently ignores errors when columns already exist.
   - **lineItems validation**: Removed `.min(1)` requirement from `lineItems` in the schema so blueprint quotes (which have no line items) pass validation. UI still enforces at least one item for line-items type quotes.
   - **Blueprint state in FormData**: State-managed arrays (`deliverables`, `nextSteps`) are set via `formData.set()` in `handleSubmit`; plain string/number fields use HTML `name` attributes and are captured automatically by `new FormData(form)`.
   - **Admin password**: App reads from `ADMIN_PASSWORD` env var. No `.env.local` exists, so admin login is blocked. Solution: create `.env.local` with `ADMIN_PASSWORD=yourpassword`.

6. All User Messages:
   - "Implement the following plan: [full plan text for Quote UI Improvements & Blueprint Quote Type]"
   - `/commit-commands:commit` (via skill invocation)
   - "push to github"
   - "i want to see the quote UI on localhost"
   - "what is the admin password?"
   - "Your task is to create a detailed summary..." (current message)

7. Pending Tasks:
   - Create `.env.local` with `ADMIN_PASSWORD` so the admin panel is accessible (user was advised but hasn't confirmed they did it)
   - No other explicit pending tasks

8. Current Work:
   The last substantive work was answering the user's question about the admin password. Found that `src/features/auth/lib/auth.ts` reads `process.env.ADMIN_PASSWORD` and no `.env*` file exists in the project. Advised user to run:
   ```bash
   echo 'ADMIN_PASSWORD=yourpassword' > /Users/santiagocoronado/PRAGMA/pragma-web/.env.local
   ```
   and restart the dev server. The dev server is running at `http://localhost:3000`.

9. Optional Next Step:
   The user's last question was "what is the admin password?" — answered by pointing to the missing `.env.local`. The natural next step is helping the user create `.env.local` so they can log into the admin panel and see the quote UI at `http://localhost:3000/en/admin`. No explicit follow-up request has been made yet, so no next step should be initiated without user confirmation.

one more bug Next.js

## Error Type
Recoverable Error

## Error Message
Hydration failed because the server rendered text didn't match the client. As a result this tree will be regenerated on the client. This can happen if a SSR-ed Client Component used:

- A server/client branch `if (typeof window !== 'undefined')`.
- Variable input such as `Date.now()` or `Math.random()` which changes each time it's called.
- Date formatting in a user's locale which doesn't match the server.
- External changing data without sending a snapshot of it along with the HTML.
- Invalid HTML tag nesting.

It can also happen if the client has a browser extension installed which messes with the HTML before React loaded.

https://react.dev/link/hydration-mismatch

This is a Turbopack cache staleness issue — the server rendered the old component (before we added the "Quote Type" column) while the client has the new one. No code fix needed.

Restart the dev server to clear it:

```
! rm -rf .next && npm run dev
```

content.text:
Two separate root causes:

**1. `uv` is not installed** — hooks that call `uv run ...` all fail with "command not found". `uv` is only present as a plugin shim, not as a real binary.

**2. `.zshenv` has a broken line** — `. "$HOME/.cargo/env"` runs on every shell invocation but `~/.cargo/env` doesn't exist, causing the warning you see in every Bash output.

**Fix 1 — Install uv:**
```
! curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh
```

This installs `uv` to `~/.local/bin/uv` (or `~/.cargo/bin/uv`).

**Fix 2 — Make hooks find uv after install.** Claude Code hooks run in `/bin/sh` and don't source your `.zshrc`, so even after installing, the PATH won't include `~/.local/bin`. You'll need to update every `uv run` hook in `settings.json` to use the full path, e.g.:

```
/Users/santiagocoronado/.local/bin/uv run ~/.claude/scripts/log-hook.py pre
```

**Fix 3 — Stop the `.zshenv` warning** by making the cargo source conditional:

Files and Code Sections: - **`src/messages/en.json`** - Added two missing keys to the `Admin` namespace that `QuoteForm.tsx` was trying to resolve: ```json "deliverables": "Deliverables", "nextSteps": "Next Steps", ``` - These were inserted near their related siblings (`deliverableTitle`, `nextStep`) - **`src/messages/es.json`** - Same additions to `Admin` namespace: ```json "deliverables": "Entregables", "nextSteps": "Siguientes Pasos", ``` - **`src/features/quotes/components/QuoteTable.tsx`** - Added a "Quote Type" column header between Client and Amount - Fixed amount display to use `fixedPrice` for blueprint quotes instead of always calling `calculateTotal(lineItems, discount)` which returned 0 for blueprints ```tsx {t("quoteType")} // ... {quote.quoteType === "blueprint" ? t("blueprintType") : t("lineItemsType")} {quote.quoteType === "blueprint" ? formatCurrency(quote.fixedPrice ?? 0, quote.currency, locale) : formatCurrency(calculateTotal(quote.lineItems, quote.discount), quote.currency, locale)} ``` - **`~/.zshenv`** - Was: `. | URL | File |

| `/en/quote/[id]` | `src/app/[locale]/quote/[id]/page.tsx` |

There's also:
- `src/app/[locale]/admin/layout.tsx` — shared layout for all admin pages
- `src/app/[locale]/layout.tsx` — shared layout for all locale pages
- `src/app/api/dev/seed/route.ts` — `GET /api/dev/seed`
- `src/app/api/quote/[id]/pdf/route.ts` — `GET /api/quote/[id]/pdf`
